scenario: A long survey form has 30 fields. The team wants to visually separate the fields into thematic sections with section titles (e.g., "Personal Info," "Contact Details," "Preferences") within the same form (no multi-step wizard).
question: Which Forms feature fits?

options.text: Text view element (Markdown-powered) used as section dividers with # Heading or ## Sub-heading Markdown syntax, optionally with Group elements providing visual containers with a Group label and Show outline toggle. There is NO standalone "Heading" element in the Camunda Forms library; the Text view with Markdown headings is the documented sectioning approach for single-page forms.

Hidden Field components store data values but have no visual rendering. They appear as hidden inputs in the form output, not as visible section headers or dividers. Using Hidden Fields as section markers would add data fields with no visible effect, cluttering the form schema without providing any visual sectioning benefit for users. 0 in Camunda 8.

Camunda Forms abstracts away the underlying HTML rendering layer. Direct raw HTML <h2> tags are not accepted as form element content or configuration. The Forms schema uses structured JSON elements with defined component types; raw HTML is outside the component model and would not render as intended inside the Forms renderer. Understanding this characteristic is essential for the Camunda 8 Certified Professional examination.

The Text view component renders Markdown including heading syntax, the Group element provides visual containers with labels and optional outlines, and these two features together provide comprehensive visual sectioning within a single-page form. Teams building on Camunda 8 should consider this when designing process models for production deployment.

a.text: Correct. "Text view" + Markdown headings is the documented sectioning idiom; for visual containers use the Group element (nesting + Show outline). NO dedicated "Heading" element exists in the Forms library.

b.text: Incorrect. Hidden Field stores data, not displays headings.

c.text: Incorrect. Forms abstracts HTML; raw HTML isn't input.

d.text: Incorrect. Sectioning achievable via Heading components.

explanation: "Text view" + Markdown headings is the documented sectioning idiom; for visual containers use the Group element (nesting + Show outline). NO dedicated "Heading" element exists in the Forms library.
